first_defeat.speaker: Varn
first_defeat.text: The chains... are quiet now. For the first time in longer than I can remember. I do not know if that is peace or dissolution. I suppose I am about to find out.

fought_well.speaker: Varn
fought_well.text: So did you. Remember what I told you — about structure, about what holds the chaos back. Someone must still hold that line. I hope it is you.

brought_it.speaker: Varn
brought_it.text: Yes. I did. That was the shape of this from the moment I chose the chains over compromise. I knew it then. I know it now. Go.

is_it_over.speaker: Varn
is_it_over.text: For me. Not for the dungeon. The chaos does not stop because I have. Someone else will have to hold the line. Perhaps you already are.

ng_plus_defeat.speaker: Varn
ng_plus_defeat.text: Again. I have fallen again. How many times have we stood here, I wonder. Does it become easier for you, each time?

responses.text: You keep making the same choice.
Get up. I know you'll be back.

ng_never_easier.speaker: Varn
ng_never_easier.text: Good. If it did, something important would have been lost. The dungeon will return me to you. I hope, next time, one of us chooses differently.

ng_same_choice.speaker: Varn
ng_same_choice.text: I know. I have wondered about that myself. The chains do not leave much room for deviation. But perhaps — this time — something will be different when I wake.

ng_back.speaker: Varn
ng_back.text: Yes. I will be back. That is the one certainty the dungeon has left me. I will try to remember more of this, next time. I always try.
